articles.headline: Why are there hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, and why has FIFA been criticised?
articles.description: All 104 matches at the 2026 World Cup are stopping for two hydration breaks. Why have they been stipulated by FIFA, and why have they been criticised?

images.name:
Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.alt: Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium

images.name: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium [1296x729]
images.caption: Argentina's Enzo Fernández said he felt "dizzy" while playing in "very dangerous" temperatures during last summer's Club World Cup in the United States.
images.alt: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium

images.name:
Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Advertising is prominent on stadium screens during drinks breaks at the World Cup.
images.alt: Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium

images.name:
Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Virgil van Dijk has been critical of the hydration breaks introduced at this summer's World Cup.
images.alt: Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium

images.name:
The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti was thankful for the opportunity to speak to his players when Brazil found themselves a goal behind to Morocco in the first half of their World Cup opener.
images.alt: The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium

images.name:
Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Hydration breaks provide a window for coaches to gather their players and tweak their tactics.
images.alt: Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia

images.name:
Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Drinks breaks could have a major effect on the outcome of matches at the World Cup.
images.alt: Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
images.credit: Kirill KUDRYAVTSEV / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Would Amorim at Milan be good news for Christian Pulisic?
articles.description: Would Amorim at Milan be good news for Christian Pulisic?

images.name: Would Amorim at Milan be good news for Christian Pulisic?
images.caption: Would Amorim at Milan be good news for Christian Pulisic?

articles.headline: Spain at the men's World Cup: All-time results and stats
articles.description: Take a look at the Spain men's national team's performance throughout the tournament's history.

articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: Cape Verde hanging tough vs. heavily favored Spain
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Spain v Cape Verde Pedri Kevin Pina [1296x729]
Moreno: Signing Cucurella would make sense for Real Madrid
images.caption: Moreno: Signing Cucurella would make sense for Real Madrid

articles.headline: Dider Deschamps backs Ousmane Dembele to find PSG form with France
articles.description: France head coach Didier Deschamps has backed his Ballon d'Or-winning forward Ousmane Dembélé to begin to show his Paris Saint-Germain form with the national side at the FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Ousmane Dembele [1296x729]
images.caption: Ousmane Dembele recently won the UEFA Champions League with PSG, and is under pressure to replicate his club form with France at the FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Paul Pogba's wholesome reaction to Zidane signing a France shirt
images.caption: Paul Pogba's wholesome reaction to Zidane signing a France shirt

articles.headline: France head coach Didier Deschamps says 'there's no revenge in football' ahead of Senegal rematch at FIFA World Cup
articles.description: France head coach Didier Deschamps insists that revenge for their 2002 loss to Senegal is not in his side's sights as they approach Tuesday's rematch at the FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Didier Deschamps [1296x729]
images.caption: Didier Deschamps directs traffic at France's training session ahead of their opening FIFA World Cup clash against Senegal.
images.credit: FRANCK FIFE / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Onuoha praises Diomande's impact in Ivory Coast's win over Ecuador
images.caption: Onuoha praises Diomande's impact in Ivory Coast's win over Ecuador

article.headline: Colombia win to top Group H and eliminate Senegal on fair play
article.description: Colombia defeated Senegal 1-0 to advance to the World Cup round of 16 as Group H winners in a result that sent the African side out on fair play.
article.linkText: Colombia win; Senegal out on fair play

images.name: Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [1296x729]

peers.name: Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [1296x1296]
Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [1296x729]
Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [864x1296]
Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [1296x864]
Senegal vs. Colombia (COL Yerry Mina celebrates) 6/28/2018 [1296x518]

article.story:
Colombia defeated Senegal 1-0 to advance to the World Cup round of 16 as Group H winners in a result that sent the African side out of the competition on the fair play ranking.

Yerry Mina's 74th-minute header allowed Colombia to top the group on six points, while Senegal finished level on four points with Japan, who lost 1-0 to already-eliminated Poland in the other group game played simultaneously.

Both Japan and Senegal had even goal differences and had scored four goals each, but Japan advanced as runners-up because they only picked up four yellow cards in the group stage, while Senegal had six.

The fair play ranking is being used as a tiebreaker for the first time at a World Cup. Previously, a random drawing of lots would have decided which team would advance.

Senegal, who had most of the better play in the first half, thought they had a penalty when Sadio Mane went down in the area on 17 minutes under a challenge from Davinson Sanchez.

However, after consultation with the video assistant referee, referee Milorad Mazic deemed Sanchez to have touched the ball and the decision was reversed.

Chances throughout the game were thin on the ground, with Colombia striker Radamel Falcao heading off target in the 25th minute and again in the second half, while substitute Luis Muriel had an effort blocked.

After Poland took the lead over Japan in the other game, a draw would have seen both Colombia and Senegal through, but Mina rose highest to head in Juan Quintero's corner to put the South American side ahead.

It wasn't all good news for Colombia, however, as talismanic forward James Rodriguez left the game in the first half with what appeared to be a recurrence of the calf injury that kept him out of their opening game.

Colombia will face the runners-up of Group G, either England or Belgium, who face off later on Thursday and whose ranking will also be decided via fair play should they draw.

Senegal lost a World Cup group-stage game for the first time in their history. Their elimination means it will be the first time since 1982 that there have been no African teams in the round of 16.

Information from Reuters was used in this report.

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup
